plan_adherence_notes: Diffing this branch against its `futarchy-fleet` merge base (`f61003e154cdfe0a0fc519d2efe75b278612e181`) shows only `.fleet/tasks` artifact changes. The task-local `questions.json` was never created, there are no committed edits to `backend/server.py` or `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`, and the planned route-breadth HTTP coverage never landed. The live code audit still matches the researched shared seam, but the execution steps described in the plan were not carried out on this branch.
missed_questions: Which exact pytest cases or node ids count as the "new route-breadth tests" that should gate any backend patch in this step?
If those breadth tests are absent from the checkout, is this step supposed to add them or stop after recording audit-only evidence?
What minimal valid HTTP fixtures should be standardized for comment-post and event-trade so all five protected POST routes are exercised through `BayesHandler.handle_api()`?
reason: The research is largely correct: `resolve_write_route_category(...)` still classifies exactly the five protected POST routes, `_enforce_write_controls()` still enforces before routing, and `BayesHandler.handle_api()` plus `rate_limit_headers(...)` remain the only quota-header emission path, with no route-local `X-RateLimit-*` writes. Current backend behavior also matches that contract: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_bayes_market_api.py -k BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests -q` passed (`18 passed, 212 deselected`), and an ad hoc HTTP probe showed create-market, comment-post, market-resolve, probability-edit, and event-trade each returning `201` with the full `X-RateLimit-*` set when limiting is enabled. But the branch never executed the planned durable work: it adds only `.fleet/tasks` files, it is missing the task-local `questions.json`, and it does not commit the promised route-breadth tests or any backend patch. Reject this branch until the breadth coverage is added or the step is explicitly re-scoped to audit-only evidence.
